title: Verify browser OAuth cart and checkout flow
verify: Real browser completes Google and VK provider-double redirects into signed session and current customer state.
Guest cart merges through the existing handoff before checkout; merge conflict blocks and retry recovers without losing source/session.
Guest/payment gate, safe return path, callback URL cleanup, cancel/failure/replay, session expiry, and logout cleanup are buyer-visible and deterministic.
Browser storage, URL, console, network evidence, screenshots, and reports contain no token, secret, session ID, raw provider payload, customer email, or production data.
evidence_required: Playwright trace/report for both provider-double paths and checkout gate.
Cart merge success/conflict/retry and logout assertions.
Artifact privacy/secret scan plus full storefront regression/build output.
HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and rollback/recovery evidence required by T3 policy.
constraints: Use real browser, storefront, Medusa, PostgreSQL, and local provider doubles.
Use existing FT-003 cart merge endpoint and state handoff.
Do not use live Google/VK accounts, production secrets, or production data.
invariants: Callback and return navigation never become open redirects.
Logout clears session and local cart reference while preserving durable backend records.
purpose: Prove the complete buyer-visible login-before-payment and cart-continuity behavior in a real browser without live providers.
success_outcome: Google/VK provider-double flows establish session, merge cart, enforce checkout gate, recover safely, and log out without credential or PII leakage.
anti_goals: Do not use live provider accounts or production data.
Do not alter backend auth/cart behavior from acceptance code.
Do not implement checkout fields, orders, inventory, or payments.

stop_conditions: E2E cannot use real Medusa/PostgreSQL and browser boundaries.
Provider double bypasses the production callback/session path.
Artifacts cannot be produced without exposing secrets, tokens, session IDs, or PII.
